skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Video outpainting on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — extend the spatial canvas of a video, change aspect ratio (9:16 vertical to 16:9 horizontal or vice versa), add environment beyond the original frame while preserving the central action. Routes prompt-shaped spatial extension through Wan 2-7 edit-video and points the agent at dedicated ComfyUI outpaint workflows when seam quality matters for hero delivery. Triggers on "video outpaint", "video outpainting", "extend video canvas", "expand video frame", "uncrop video", "aspect ratio change", "vertical to horizontal video", "16:9 from 9:16", "TikTok to YouTube", or any explicit ask to extend a video spatially beyond its original frame.
skill.summary: Extend a video canvas, change aspect ratio, and add environmental context to video frames using AI-powered outpainting via RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 64 potential security patterns (33 external_commands, 22 network, 2 filesystem) requiring AI evaluation. After semantic review of the SKILL.md file, all detected patterns are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' detections are the runcomfy CLI invocations with hardcoded arguments - a standard legitimate pattern for CLI-based skills. The 'network' detections are hardcoded RunComfy URLs for documentation and service endpoints - standard for integration skills. The 'filesystem' detections at line 148 reference the token storage path `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` which is the documented standard for token storage - not hidden file access. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections refer to MD5 in URL query parameters for tracking (utm_source) - used for analytics attribution, not security. No prompt injection attempts, no malicious intent, no actual security risks confirmed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs for Documentation and Service Endpoints
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 22 hardcoded URLs in SKILL.md. These are legitimate RunComfy documentation links, model catalogs, and service endpoints required for proper skill functionality. No data exfiltration risk as these are standard integration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged URLs are RunComfy service endpoints and documentation links - standard integration pattern with no exfiltration risk.

low_findings.title: MD5 Hash Usage in URL Query Parameters for Analytics
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' for MD5 usage in URLs. These are utm_source tracking parameters (e.g., ?utm_source=skills.sh) used for analytics attribution - not cryptographic security. MD5 is acceptable for non-security tracking purposes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 in utm parameters is standard analytics tracking - not cryptographic security. No sensitive data protected by cryptography.

low_findings.title: CLI Tool Invocations with Hardcoded Arguments
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 33 'external_commands' for runcomfy CLI invocations. These are standard CLI patterns with hardcoded arguments and JSON-structured input. No user input injection surfaces exist as JSON is passed via --input flag without shell expansion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All CLI commands use hardcoded arguments - standard pattern for CLI-based skills with no injection risk.

low_findings.title: Documented Token Storage Path for CLI Authentication
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'hidden file access' for ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. This is the documented standard token storage path for the runcomfy CLI - not malicious hidden file access. The Security & Privacy section explicitly documents this behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json is documented standard behavior - not hidden file access.

content.user_title: Extend Video Canvas and Change Aspect Ratio with AI
content.value_statement: Content creators need to adapt videos for different platforms and aspect ratios, but manual outpainting is time-consuming. This skill uses AI to intelligently extend video frames while preserving the original action.
actual_capabilities: Extend video canvas in any direction (left, right, top, bottom) using AI-powered spatial outpainting
Convert between aspect ratios (9:16 to 16:9 or vice versa) while preserving central action
Add environmental context beyond the original frame using natural language prompts
Route to Wan 2-7 edit-video for standard quality or ComfyUI workflows for hero-grade seam quality
Preserve original subject framing and central action throughout the extension
Handle TikTok/Reels to YouTube conversions and other social media format changes
limitations: Wan 2-7 Edit-Video may show quality variance at the seam - ComfyUI workflows needed for hero delivery
Multi-step outpainting degrades quality after 2 passes - limited to approximately 60% canvas expansion
Does not perform image outpainting, temporal video extension, or mask-driven inpainting - separate skills required
Generated files capped at 2 GiB by the CLI to prevent excessive downloads

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Repurposing
use_cases.description: Convert vertical TikTok or Instagram Reels videos to horizontal 16:9 YouTube format. Add matching environment on sides while preserving the original creator's content in the center.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Brand Campaign Hero Ad Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare video assets for hero ad delivery with strict seam quality requirements. Use ComfyUI LTX 2-3 outpainting workflows for temporal consistency and motion-aware fill.

use_cases.title: Legacy Footage Modernization
use_cases.description: Convert old 4:3 footage to modern 16:9 format for consistent brand presentation. Handle archival content that needs environmental context added during the conversion.
use_cases.target_user: Video archivists and media production teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Aspect Ratio Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas to 16:9 horizontal by adding matching environment on the left and right sides. Preserve the original action and subject framing in the center.
prompt_templates.scenario: When converting a vertical video to horizontal format

prompt_templates.title: Directional Canvas Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas downward to show more ground. Match the existing background style, lighting, and camera distance throughout the clip.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to add floor or ground space to a scene

prompt_templates.title: Social Media Platform Adaptation
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this TikTok video to YouTube format by adding approximately 30% environment on each side. Match the existing background style and lighting. Preserve the original creator's content in the center third.
prompt_templates.scenario: When repurposing content across multiple platforms

prompt_templates.title: Hero Delivery with Seam Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas to 16:9 with strict seam quality requirements. Ensure temporal consistency and motion-aware fill. Prepare the output for hero ad delivery with professional seam handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: When seam quality is critical for professional delivery

output_examples.input: Convert a 9:16 vertical cooking video to 16:9 horizontal YouTube format, adding matching kitchen environment on the sides
output_examples.output: A 16:9 horizontal video with the original cooking content preserved in the center third, surrounded by AI-generated kitchen environment that matches the original lighting and style. Seamless transition at the frame edges.

output_examples.input: Extend a 4:3 interview footage to 16:9, adding background environment that matches the studio setting
output_examples.output: A widescreen 16:9 video where the original 4:3 content remains centered, with AI-generated studio background extending to fill the new aspect ratio. Consistent lighting and depth maintained throughout.

best_practices: Lead with the canvas change in your prompt: specify the exact aspect ratio or extension direction
End prompts with preservation instructions: 'Preserve the original action and subject framing in the center' to prevent restyling
Use ComfyUI workflows for hero ad delivery when seam quality is critical - Route 1 is for acceptable seam quality
anti_patterns: Do not request multi-step outpainting beyond 2 passes - quality degrades significantly
Do not use this skill for image outpainting or temporal video extension - use separate skills
Do not attempt to outpaint without preserving central action - Wan 2-7 may restyle original content

faq.question: What is video outpainting?
faq.answer: Video outpainting extends the spatial canvas of a video beyond its original frame. It adds new content to the sides, top, or bottom of the video while preserving the original content in the center.

faq.question: What is the difference between outpainting and inpainting?
faq.answer: Outpainting adds new content beyond the original frame edges. Inpainting modifies content inside the frame, often using masks to target specific areas. This skill handles outpainting only.

faq.question: Can I convert TikTok videos to YouTube format?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the Wan 2-7 Edit-Video route with a prompt like 'Extend the canvas to 16:9 horizontal by adding matching environment on the left and right sides. Preserve the original action in the center.'

faq.question: When should I use ComfyUI workflows instead of the CLI?
faq.answer: Use ComfyUI workflows for hero ad delivery with strict seam quality requirements. The CLI handles most social media and general use cases well, but hero delivery needs dedicated ComfyUI outpainting graphs.

faq.question: How much can I extend a video canvas?
faq.answer: A single pass extends approximately 30% on each side. You can run two passes for up to 60% total expansion, but quality degrades after the second pass.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill uses the runcomfy CLI which accepts video URLs. The CLI supports common video formats and handles download to the specified output directory.
